decision_summary: The current design is feasible with a chip max temperature of ~58.6°C, well below the 85.0°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader width to enhance lateral heat distribution towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increasing the spreader width maximizes the cross-sectional area for lateral heat conduction towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries, reducing thermal resistance.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader
risk_notes: The spreader width is approaching the design domain limit (1.2m); future geometry changes may be limited.
Diminishing returns may occur as the spreader approaches the full width of the cooling boundaries.
